title: heliotropium undulatum subsp. antiatlanticum
intro: Heliotropium undulatum subsp. antiatlanticum, also known as the Cape heliotrope or blue heliotrope, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Heliotropium undulatum subsp. antiatlanticum include its ability to reduce inflammation, treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
